text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ATTENDING ONCOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Medical Oncology
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [DATE]
REPORT DATE: [DATE]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, RIGHT UPPER LOBE PULMONARY NODULE
  - Submitted in formalin, labeled "RUL lung nodule biopsy"
  - Received in adequate condition
  - 3 cores of tan-pink soft tissue, aggregate 1.4 cm in length; individual core lengths 0.4-0.6 cm

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The patient is a 58-year-old male with known Stage IV (AJCC 8th edition, cT4aN2bM1a) KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS, moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon diagnosed approximately 10 months ago. Initial staging revealed synchronous bilobar hepatic metastases (segments II, IV, VI, VII). He has been treated with first-line mFOLFOX6 plus bev (12 cycles, partial response), followed by maintenance LV5FU2 plus bev. Recent restaging CT (prompted by rising CEA to 94.7 ng/mL and CA 19-9 to 287 U/mL) demonstrated radiographic progression: growth of known hepatic metastases and development of new bilateral pulmonary nodules (right upper lobe 8 mm, right lower lobe 6 mm, left upper lobe 7 mm). Biopsy of the right upper lobe nodule is performed to confirm metastatic origin and exclude a synchronous primary lung malignancy, given the patient's 20-pack-year smoking history.

Clinical impression prior to biopsy: Metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma versus primary lung adenocarcinoma.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "RUL lung nodule biopsy," are three tan-pink, cylindrical core biopsy fragments measuring 0.6 cm, 0.5 cm, and 0.4 cm in length, each approximately 0.1 cm in diameter. The aggregate tissue measures 1.4 cm. The cores are soft to firm, without grossly identifiable necrosis or hemorrhage. All tissue submitted entirely in one cassette (A1).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Histologic sections of the core biopsy material reveal fragments of pulmonary parenchyma infiltrated by a malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor is arranged in irregular glandular structures with complex, cribriform, and back-to-back architecture. The neoplastic glands are lined by columnar to cuboidal cells with pseudostratified, hyperchromatic, elongated nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate-to-abundant pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Nuclear pleomorphism is moderate. Mitotic figures are readily identified, including atypical forms. Intraluminal necrotic debris ("dirty necrosis") is present within multiple glandular spaces, a feature highly characteristic of colorectal adenocarcinoma. There is no evidence of acinar formation with lepidic spread, papillary architecture, or mucin-secreting goblet cell morphology that would suggest a primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma.

The background lung parenchyma shows reactive type II pneumocyte hyperplasia and mild interstitial fibrosis. There is no evidence of an in-situ lepidic component. No granulomas are identified. No fungal organisms or acid-fast bacilli are seen on special stains.

The overall morphology is most consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma from a colorectal primary.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A, block A1):

  Tumor marker panel for adenocarcinoma of unknown primary vs. colorectal origin:

  CDX2 (caudal-type homeobox 2):         DIFFUSELY AND STRONGLY POSITIVE
                                          (3+ nuclear staining in >95% of tumor cells)

  CK20 (cytokeratin 20):                 POSITIVE
                                          (diffuse cytoplasmic staining, 3+)

  CK7 (cytokeratin 7):                   FOCALLY POSITIVE
                                          (weak cytoplasmic staining, 1+, in approximately
                                          20% of tumor cells)

  TTF-1 (thyroid transcription factor-1): NEGATIVE
                                          (no nuclear staining in tumor cells; positive
                                          internal control in background pneumocytes)

  Napsin A:                              NEGATIVE
                                          (no granular cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells;
                                          positive internal control in background
                                          pneumocytes)

  p40:                                   NEGATIVE
                                          (excludes squamous cell carcinoma)

  SATB2:                                 POSITIVE
                                          (nuclear staining in majority of tumor cells,
                                          further supporting colorectal origin)

IHC INTERPRETATION: The immunohistochemical profile (CDX2 diffusely positive, CK20 positive, CK7 focally positive, TTF-1 negative, Napsin A negative, p40 negative, SATB2 positive) is consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin. This profile effectively excludes primary lung adenocarcinoma (which would be expected to be TTF-1 and/or Napsin A positive, with CK7 positivity and CDX2 negativity). The focal CK7 positivity is a recognized minor finding in a subset of colorectal adenocarcinomas and does not alter the interpretation.

MOLECULAR / LIQUID BIOPSY (reported separately; referenced here for clinical correlation):
  Concurrent ctDNA (circulating tumor DNA) analysis performed at time of progression:
  - KRAS p.G12D mutation confirmed (variant allele frequency 3.8%)
  - Results consistent with the known molecular profile of the patient's colorectal primary
  - Full liquid biopsy report filed separately under accession [REDACTED]

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) IHC: PENDING
  (Repeat MMR IHC ordered on this specimen per clinical request; results to follow in addendum)
  NOTE: Prior testing on sigmoid primary (colonoscopic biopsy) demonstrated intact MMR protein expression (MLH1+, MSH2+, MSH6+, PMS2+); MSS by PCR.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A - CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, RIGHT UPPER LOBE PULMONARY NODULE:

  METASTATIC MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH
  COLORECTAL PRIMARY.

  - Morphology: columnar glandular architecture with intraluminal dirty necrosis
    and nuclear pseudostratification consistent with colorectal origin.
  - Immunophenotype: CDX2+, CK20+, CK7 focally+, TTF-1-, Napsin A-, SATB2+;
    primary lung adenocarcinoma excluded.
  - No evidence of an in-situ or lepidic growth component to suggest primary
    pulmonary origin.
  - No granulomas or infectious organisms identified.
  - Correlation with clinical history of Stage IV KRAS G12D-mutant sigmoid
    adenocarcinoma with known hepatic metastases is recommended and supportive.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The histomorphologic and immunohistochemical findings are diagnostic of metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma in this pulmonary nodule. The IHC profile, particularly diffuse CDX2 and CK20 positivity combined with negative TTF-1 and Napsin A, is highly specific for colorectal origin and effectively excludes a primary lung adenocarcinoma. SATB2 positivity provides additional confirmatory support for lower gastrointestinal primary origin. The focal CK7 staining is within the expected range of minor co-expression seen in a subset of colorectal carcinomas and is not sufficient to suggest a CK7-predominant primary.

In the context of this patient's 20-pack-year smoking history, the exclusion of a synchronous primary lung malignancy is clinically significant and supports re-staging as metastatic colorectal cancer with pulmonary involvement rather than a second primary.

MMR IHC results on this specimen are pending and will be reported in an addendum when available.

Co-signed by:
Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], MD
Department of Anatomic Pathology

Resident/Fellow co-signatory: [REDACTED], MD

*** ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED ***

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM (PENDING - TO BE ISSUED UPON COMPLETION OF MMR IHC)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
